differential_diagnoses: Lithium toxicity (despite low serum level, intracellular effects possible)
next_steps: Obtain thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, total T3, TPO antibodies, TSH-receptor antibodies)
Perform lumbar puncture for autoimmune encephalitis panel (CSF NMDA receptor antibodies)
Initiate continuous EEG monitoring to rule out non-convulsive status epilepticus
Monitor vital signs closely for thyroid storm/autonomic instability
Consider MRI of the thyroid or radioactive iodine uptake scan if labs confirm hyperthyroidism
Maintain benzodiazepine taper and supportive care

differential_diagnoses: Thyrotoxicosis (e.g., Graves' disease or subacute thyroiditis)
Hashimoto's encephalopathy (Steroid-responsive encephalopathy associated with autoimmune thyroiditis)
next_steps: Obtain emergent thyroid function panel including TSH, free T4, and total T3
Order thyroid peroxidase (TPO) antibodies and TSH-receptor antibodies
Initiate continuous EEG monitoring to rule out ongoing non-convulsive status epilepticus
Administer intravenous thiamine, folate, and multivitamin (B-complex) supplementation
Consult endocrinology for evaluation of potential thyroid storm or autoimmune thyroiditis
Consider high-dose corticosteroids if Hashimoto's encephalopathy is suspected after excluding infectious etiologies
Maintain strict seizure precautions and monitor for worsening autonomic instability
